response: Data collection begins with defining the study objectives, ensuring data relevance to the health issue under investigation. Data sources must be identified and validated to ensure data accuracy and reliability. Data is gathered through surveys, interviews, or medical records, with each data point carefully documented. Data analysis techniques are selected based on the type of data collected and the research questions. Data interpretation involves examining patterns within the data to identify potential risk factors. Data is cross-referenced with existing data sets to strengthen the study's findings. Data discrepancies are reviewed to ensure consistency across all data entries. Data is then compiled into reports that summarize key findings and highlight the significance of the data collected.
